heading: Guarantees for bonds and notes issued for community or economic development purposes
label: 4713a Guarantees for bonds and notes issued for community or economic development purposes

children.heading: Eligible community development financial institution
children.label: 1 Eligible community development financial institution
children.text: The term “eligible community development financial institution” means a community development financial institution (as described in section 1805.201 of title 12, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor thereto) certified by the Secretary that has applied to a qualified issuer for, or been granted by a qualified issuer, a loan under the Program.

children.heading: Eligible community or economic development purpose
children.label: 2 Eligible community or economic development purpose
A
children.text: means any purpose described in ; andsection 4707(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: includes the provision of community or economic development in low-income or underserved rural areas.

children.heading: Guarantee
children.label: 3 Guarantee
children.text: The term “guarantee” means a written agreement between the Secretary and a qualified issuer (or trustee), pursuant to which the Secretary ensures repayment of the verifiable losses of principal, interest, and call premium, if any, on notes or bonds issued by a qualified issuer to finance or refinance loans to eligible community development financial institutions.

children.heading: Loan
children.label: 4 Loan
children.text: The term “loan” means any credit instrument that is extended under the Program for any eligible community or economic development purpose.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “master servicer” means any entity approved by the Secretary in accordance with subparagraph (B) to oversee the activities of servicers, as provided in subsection (f)(4).

children.heading: Approval criteria for master servicers
children.label: B Approval criteria for master servicers
i
children.text: loan administration, servicing, and loan monitoring;

children.label: ii
children.text: managing regional or national loan intake, processing, or servicing operational systems and infrastructure;

children.label: iii
children.text: managing regional or national originator communication systems and infrastructure;

children.label: iv
children.text: developing and implementing training and other risk management strategies on a regional or national basis; and

children.label: v
children.text: compliance monitoring, investor relations, and reporting.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 6 Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the guarantee Program for bonds and notes issued for eligible community or economic development purposes established under this section.

children.heading: Program administrator
children.label: 7 Program administrator
children.text: The term “Program administrator” means an entity designated by the issuer to perform administrative duties, as provided in subsection (f)(2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “qualified issuer” means a community development financial institution (or any entity designated to issue notes or bonds on behalf of such community development financial institution) that meets the qualification requirements of this paragraph.

children.heading: Approval criteria for qualified issuers
children.label: B Approval criteria for qualified issuers

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall approve a qualified issuer for a guarantee under the Program in accordance with the requirements of this paragraph, and such additional requirements as the Secretary may establish, by regulation.

children.label: I
children.text: have appropriate expertise, capacity, and experience, or otherwise be qualified to make loans for eligible community or economic development purposes;

children.label: aa
children.text: an acceptable statement of the proposed sources and uses of the funds; and

children.label: bb
children.text: a capital distribution plan that meets the requirements of subsection (c)(1); and

children.label: III
children.text: certify to the Secretary that the bonds or notes to be guaranteed are to be used for eligible community or economic development purposes.

children.heading: Department opinion
children.label: i Department opinion
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date of a request by a qualified issuer for approval of a guarantee under the Program, the Secretary shall provide an opinion regarding compliance by the issuer with the requirements of the Program under this section.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: ii Timing
children.text: The Secretary shall approve or deny a guarantee under this section after consideration of the opinion provided to the Secretary under clause (i), and in no case later than 90 days after receipt of all required information by the Secretary with respect to a request for such guarantee.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 9 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Servicer
children.label: 10 Servicer
children.text: The term “servicer” means an entity designated by the issuer to perform various servicing duties, as provided in subsection (f)(3).
for eligible community or economic development purposes; or
to refinance loans or notes issued for such purposes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A capital distribution plan meets the requirements of this subsection, if not less than 90 percent of the principal amount of guaranteed bonds or notes (other than costs of issuance fees) are used to make loans for any eligible community or economic development purpose, measured annually, beginning at the end of the 1-year period beginning on the issuance date of such guaranteed bonds or notes.

children.heading: Relending account
children.label: 2 Relending account
children.text: Not more than 10 percent of the principal amount of guaranteed bonds or notes, multiplied by an amount equal to the outstanding principal balance of issued notes or bonds, minus the risk-share pool amount under subsection (d), may be held in a relending account and may be made available for new eligible community or economic development purposes.

children.label: A
children.text: community or economic development loans;

children.label: B
children.text: a relending account, to the extent authorized under paragraph (2); or

children.label: C
children.text: a risk-share pool established under subsection (d).

children.heading: Repayment
children.label: 4 Repayment
children.text: If a qualified issuer fails to meet the requirements of paragraph (1) by the end of the 90-day period beginning at the end of the annual measurement period, repayment shall be made on that portion of bonds or notes necessary to bring the bonds or notes that remain outstanding after such repayment into compliance with the 90 percent requirement of paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: prohibit, as appropriate, certain uses of amounts from the guarantee of a bond or note under the Program, including the use of such funds for political activities, lobbying, outreach, counseling services, or travel expenses; and

children.label: i
children.text: the qualified issuer; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any recipient of amounts from the guarantee of a bond or note.

children.heading: Risk-share pool
children.label: d Risk-share pool
children.text: Each qualified issuer shall, during the term of a guarantee provided under the Program, establish a risk-share pool, capitalized by contributions from eligible community development financial institution participants an amount equal to 3 percent of the guaranteed amount outstanding on the subject notes and bonds.

children.label: A
children.text: be for the full amount of a bond or note, including the amount of principal, interest, and call premiums;

children.label: B
children.text: be fully assignable and transferable to the capital market, on terms and conditions that are consistent with comparable Government-guaranteed bonds, and satisfactory to the Secretary;

children.label: C
children.text: represent the full faith and credit of the United States; and

children.label: D
children.text: not exceed 30 years.

children.heading: Annual number of guarantees
children.label: A Annual number of guarantees
children.text: The Secretary shall issue not more than 10 guarantees in any calendar year under the Program.

children.heading: Guarantee amount
children.label: B Guarantee amount
children.text: The Secretary may not guarantee any amount under the Program equal to less than $100,000,000, but the total of all such guarantees in any fiscal year may not exceed $1,000,000,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To maximize efficiencies and minimize cost and interest rates, loans made under this section may be serviced by qualified Program administrators, bond servicers, and a master servicer.

children.label: A
children.text: approving and qualifying eligible community development financial institution applications for participation in the Program;

children.label: B
children.text: compliance monitoring;

children.label: C
children.text: bond packaging in connection with the Program; and

children.label: D
children.text: all other duties and related services that are customarily expected of a Program administrator.

children.label: A
children.text: billing and collecting loan payments;

children.label: B
children.text: initiating collection activities on past-due loans;

children.label: C
children.text: transferring loan payments to the master servicing accounts;

children.label: D
children.text: loan administration and servicing;

children.label: E
children.text: systematic and timely reporting of loan performance through remittance and servicing reports;

children.label: F
children.text: proper measurement of annual outstanding loan requirements; and

children.label: G
children.text: all other duties and related services that are customarily expected of servicers.

children.label: A
children.text: tracking the movement of funds between the accounts of the master servicer and any other servicer;

children.label: B
children.text: ensuring orderly receipt of the monthly remittance and servicing reports of the servicer;

children.label: C
children.text: monitoring the collection comments and foreclosure actions;

children.label: D
children.text: aggregating the reporting and distribution of funds to trustees and investors;

children.label: E
children.text: removing and replacing a servicer, as necessary;

children.label: F
children.text: loan administration and servicing;

children.label: G
children.text: systematic and timely reporting of loan performance compiled from all bond servicers’ reports;

children.label: H
children.text: proper distribution of funds to investors; and

children.label: I
children.text: all other duties and related services that are customarily expected of a master servicer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A qualified issuer that receives a guarantee issued under this section on a bond or note shall pay a fee to the Secretary, in an amount equal to 10 basis points of the amount of the unpaid principal of the bond or note guaranteed.

children.heading: Payment
children.label: 2 Payment
children.text: A qualified issuer shall pay the fee required under this subsection on an annual basis.

children.heading: Use of fees
children.label: 3 Use of fees
children.text: Fees collected by the Secretary under this subsection shall be used to reimburse the Department of the Treasury for any administrative costs incurred by the Department in implementing the Program established under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary, such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Use of fees
children.label: 2 Use of fees
children.text: To the extent that the amount of funds appropriated for a fiscal year under paragraph (1) are not sufficient to carry out this section, the Secretary may use the fees collected under subsection (g) for the cost of providing guarantees of bonds and notes under this section.

children.heading: Investment in guaranteed bonds ineligible for Community Reinvestment Act purposes
children.label: i Investment in guaranteed bonds ineligible for Community Reinvestment Act purposes
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any investment by a financial institution in bonds or notes guaranteed under the Program shall not be taken into account in assessing the record of such institution for purposes of the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 ( [et seq.]).12 U.S.C. 2901

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 1 Regulations
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall promulgate regulations to carry out this section.September 27, 2010

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 2 Implementation
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall implement this section.September 27, 2010

children.heading: Termination
children.label: k Termination
children.text: This section is repealed, and the authority provided under this section shall terminate, on .September 30, 20141See Termination Date note below.1
